description: M5 (Epic 5, RES-G Slice C): HU limped-pot flop thresholds + authored rationale. The RES-G §4b directions live in sizing_rules as DATA (small polar lead; mostly-check OOP; big limped-pot bets are over-repped by value; texture decides the edge from a score=0 no-baseline start) — the graders (backend/app/domain/postflop.py::grade_limped_lead / grade_limped_vs_lead) read every direction-shaping number from here, never hardcode it. All values are [DERIVED-ASSUMPTION] design targets per RES-G; EVs stay approximate (heuristic, no solver).

entries.rationale: Nobody raised preflop, so neither range is condensed: there is no aggressor edge to lean on and the board texture decides who this flop favors. When you do lead a limped pot, bet small and polar — real strength plus some air — and check the medium made hands, especially out of position, since a big bet into a limped field only gets action from the hands that beat you.

After completing the small blind you play the whole hand out of position with a capped range against a capped range: mostly check, and when you lead, keep it small and polar — strong hands and some air — because medium strength bloating a limped pot out of position is the classic way to lose a big pot with a small hand.

A limper who bets the flop is polarized around a capped range — limpers rarely hold the true top of the deck, but a BIG bet in a limped pot is over-represented by real strength. Continue with made hands and draws at a good price, fold the air, and tighten the marginal bluff-catch as the lead gets bigger.

When the big blind stabs at a limped flop it is whoever-bets-first aggression, not a preflop-range edge: defend made hands and draws getting a price, but respect large sizings — in limped pots the big bets are value-heavy — and let the marginal one-pair hands fold more as the bet grows.
